routingPolicy: Active docs consume the global Codex multi-agent lane contract, keep Codex-owned subagent effort task-flexible, and preserve historical wave provenance.
currentFocus: Keep the evidence hierarchy authoritative while Stage A Task 5 executes the accepted Level 850 identity matrix and conditionally publishes only a sanitized identity/causality result.

currentDocsTruth.release: v1.0.9 remains the latest published unsigned portable WinUI ZIP with 949 packaged Lore documents; this source slice does not publish a release.
currentDocsTruth.product: WinUI 3 is the primary preservation/modding GUI. The separate GPL rebuild has a deterministic Core/headless verifier and one playable procedural Godot .NET First Flight client; Electron, WPF, and the old Python GUI/CLI remain archived references.
currentDocsTruth.winUiFirstUse: CURRENT_CAPABILITIES.md and its byte-identical Lore mirror record the verified three-state setup hierarchy, preservation of temporarily unavailable remembered folders, manual Save Lab availability, shell status/focus ownership, Level 1 headings, compact wrapped source actions, truthful launch/Admin Level preset state, and demand-loaded Media VLC behavior.
currentDocsTruth.filesystemSafety: Save/options, AppCore copied-executable patching, and generated asset/package outputs use bounded roots, guarded or staged writes, final verification, recoverable full-file backups, and explicit same-user post-finalization limits.
currentDocsTruth.patchContract: The normative contract accounts for 29 catalog rows, 20 visible options, 9 hidden companions, 17 dependency edges, and 118 conflict edges. AppCore accepts only exact pinned mutation rows, publishes backup/checksum/apply/restore files atomically, and repairs damaged or truncated copies only from an integrity- and provenance-verified full-file backup. Standalone Python patch scripts remain lab helpers without the AppCore atomic-publication guarantee.
currentDocsTruth.re: reverse-engineering/ is canonical. The 2026-07-12 crosswalk corrects 0x004081c0 to CBattleEngine__Move, 0x00410c50 to CBattleEngineJetPart__Move, and four adjacent JetPart helpers. The 6411/6411 metric is metadata closure, not semantic completeness. Active historical probes/mutators that can reapply withdrawn CMonitor ownership remain a tooling follow-up. A dedicated user-owned Codex task owns Ghidra full-reaudit absorption, recovery, retention, correction review, and targeted revalidation; its findings remain lane-owned until reviewed integration.
currentDocsTruth.runtimeObserver: The approved design progresses from an exact-specimen Level 850 identity canary to controlled raw-state observation and then Level 100 corroboration. Tasks 1-4 provide PE32-safe fingerprints, strict sanitized materialization, exact receipt-bound CDB/input identity, the locked unpatched protocol, and authority-gated fixed-role orchestration. Task 5 corrections through active CDB log sharing are pushed at 9e438c11. Retry 7 passed its fresh no-input role with one ready marker, zero events, integrity, and cleanup, then failed closed in positiveTransform: exact-window SendInput returned false, legacy keybd_event fallback produced no canary event, SendInput did not confirm Q release, and neither positiveRepeat nor public materialization ran. An immutable private correction note narrows cleanup truth to a best-effort legacy key-up before exact teardown. Offline ABI proof found the helper's 64-bit INPUT union marshaled to 32 rather than the required 40 bytes because MOUSEINPUT/HARDWAREINPUT were absent. The current TDD correction completes and validates the x64/x86 union, records SendInput diagnostics, forbids legacy fallback as primary canary delivery, tracks standalone key-up failure truthfully, and requires exactly two successful SendInput events in the generated runner. Its 21 input-helper and 39 smoke-contract tests pass, and a fresh runner builds with zero warnings/errors. Normal review found and closed a malformed-zero consumer gap with a strict property/type/value predicate and compiled negative probe; final normal/adversarial review accepts the diff. Commit/push and fresh retry 8 remain. The sanitized schema exposes aggregate source/copy integrity booleans rather than individual source hashes. Stage A excludes timing, field semantics, handling, and parity claims.
currentDocsTruth.referenceCorpora: The pinned audit records Onslaught 792545b as an incomplete/non-buildable architecture corpus (110 tracked files; 202/254 quoted includes absent) and AYAResourceExtractor 6f3df296 as a Windows x86 static model/texture exporter with format, fixture, build, and component-license gaps. Older 111/75 manifests counted .git control files.
currentDocsTruth.lore: The 949-document package count proves packaged breadth, not editorial completeness, freshness, provenance/rights review, or broad public-safety approval.
currentDocsTruth.rebuild: The GPL-3.0-or-later RE-informed source rebuild includes the deterministic net8 Core/headless verifier, pure real-time Client adapter, and playable procedural Godot 4.7 First Flight view. Transform, energy, shield, movement, arena, and presentation choices remain synthetic; no retail-informed behavior change should land before the copied-runtime movement contract is accepted. The client needs no retail payload and is not in the published v1.0.9 WinUI ZIP.
currentDocsTruth.curatedSourcePackage: The curated artifact is a self-contained, plain non-Git convenience package over the already-public canonical repo, not a replacement checkout or a private/public legal cut. The accepted First Flight source candidate selected 721 files, materialized 726 files with export metadata, excluded the real game-generated save baseline, and passed standalone payload/inventory, docs, npm test, notices, and hygiene gates.

activeBlockers: CURRENT_CAPABILITIES.md and reverse-engineering/RE-INDEX.md remain too large for fast contributor orientation.
release/readiness/ and tools/ still need generated front-door indexes before broad movement or deletion.
Lore needs an explicit editorial allowlist, third-party-text inventory, rights/provenance review, and generated canonical projections before another release.
The grouped WinUI action/reaction map still needs a verified control-by-control refresh against the current GUI.
The movement slice still lacks copied-runtime measurements, and the AYA lane lacks a tracked public end-to-end synthetic export fixture plus resolved component-license posture.
Active BattleEngine historical probes/mutators can still reapply withdrawn CMonitor ownership; they must be retired or fail-closed before another related Ghidra mutation wave.
The AYA export orchestrator preflights only legacy AYA/DDS DLLs even though the harness also requires Fbx.dll and an FBX template.
nextSteps: Commit and push the accepted Task 5 native INPUT/exact-SendInput correction, then use fresh ignored retry-8 controls and an absent proof root for the exact Level 850 control/positive/repeat matrix.
Retire stale BattleEngine ownership tooling and add fail-fast Fbx.dll plus FBX-template preflight with focused tests.
Document and test a public synthetic AYA/DDS/FBX export contract before promoting exporter completeness.
Freeze one accepted public-safe behavior contract, then keep rebuild and GUI wording bounded to the measured result.
Generate contributor front-door indexes and run the deep Lore editorial/provenance pass after the crosswalk foundation is in place.
